title: Bar-Kooda
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image=[[File:BarKooda-BarKooda.jpg]] |name=Bar-Kooda |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]]''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Boba Fett: Bounty on Bar-Kooda]]'', by the end of which Bar-Kooda has been [[Death/Legends|killed]], as well as its sequel, ''[[Boba Fett: When the Fat Lady Swings]]'', to [[Standard decade/Legends|ten years]] after {{Film|IV}}, which corresponds to [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. Furthermore, in ''Bounty on Bar-Kooda'', [[Wim Magwit]] states that he first met Bar-Kooda at least four [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior, meaning his first encounter with the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] must have occurred by [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]]. |species=[[Herglic/Legends|Herglic]]{{CSWECite|I|62|Bar-Kooda}} |gender= |pronouns=He/him''[[Boba Fett: Bounty on Bar-Kooda]]'' |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings=[[Ry-Kooda]]''[[Boba Fett: When the Fat Lady Swings]]'' |children= |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Bar-Kooda may be a hardhearted, low-down, evil spawn of a viper, but I ain't so cruel as I'd deny an old shipmate his last request!|Bar-Kooda|}} '''Bar-Kooda''' was a notorious [[Herglic/Legends|Herglic]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] who had a reputation of [[Diet/Legends|eating]] his victims. At some point by [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], the ''[[Bloodstar]]'', the massive [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Captain/Legends|captained]] by Bar-Kooda, attacked a [[Passenger liner/Legends|spaceliner]] aboard which was the [[Magic/Legends|magician]] [[Wim Magwit]]. Bar-Kooda forced Magwit to perform in front of him and his crew of pirates and, becoming amazed by the magician's [[Magwit's Mystifying Hoop|Mystifying Hoop]] act, kept him aboard the ''Bloodstar'' for four more [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. Eventually, Magwit escaped from Bar-Kooda, but the Herglic put out a warrant for the magician's arrest, and the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] captured Magwit in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]]. However, the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Gorga Desilijic Aarrpo/Legends|Gorga Desilijic Aarrpo]] also hired Fett to [[Death/Legends|kill]] Bar-Kooda in order to please the [[Merchant/Legends|trader]] [[Orko]], with whose [[Gender/Legends|daughter]], [[Anachro]], Gorga had fallen in love. To that end, Fett forced Magwit to smuggle him aboard the ''Bloodstar'' and assist in [[Mission to the Bloodstar|capturing]] Bar-Kooda. Aboard the pirates' ship, Bar-Kooda ordered Magwit to perform the Mystifying Hoop trick once again. The magician persuaded the Herglic to stick his head through the frame that formed a part of the Hoop's [[matter transmitter]] system—only for the pirate to be pulled by the waiting Fett through the other frame located in the ''Bloodstar''{{'s}} cargo hold. Bar-Kooda engaged Fett in a brief brawl that ended when the latter knocked the former unconscious. Fett, who was carrying Bar-Kooda, and Magwit then both made their way back to the [[Escape pod/Legends|spacecraft]] on which they had arrived aboard the ''Bloodstar'' and escaped the pirate ship. Fett subsequently killed Bar-Kooda, and for a feast he was having with Orko and Anachro on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], Gorga had a roasted [[Foodstuff/Legends|entrée]] prepared out of the pirate. Bar-Kooda's older brother, [[Ry-Kooda]], later attempted to avenge his death but was also killed by Fett. ==Biography== ===Meeting a magician=== {{Quote|I was aboard a spaceliner when…when ''Bar-Kooda's'' cutthroats struck.|Wim Magwit|}} Bar-Kooda was the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] crew of the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Bloodstar]]'' and the younger [[Gender/Legends|brother]] of the [[Koodan]]{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030911hutt|text=Hutt! Hutt! Hutt!}} [[Ry-Kooda]], who resided on the [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Fferon system]] [[Planet/Legends|world]]{{CSWECite|III|115|Rutal IV}} [[Rutal IV]]. At some point by [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], Bar-Kooda's crew attacked a [[Passenger liner/Legends|spaceliner]]. The vessel had a group of entertainers aboard, all of which Bar-Kooda forced to perform for him, with the pirate [[Death/Legends|shooting]] any of the [[Artist/Legends|artists]] he did not like. [[File:BarKoodaMagwitsMystifyingHoopTrick-BarKooda.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Bar-Kooda became amazed by Wim Magwit's Mystifying Hoop trick.]] Eventually, it was the turn of the [[Magic/Legends|magician]] [[Wim Magwit]] to perform. Magwit had previously [[Thief/Legends|stolen]] a mysterious short-range teleportation device from an [[Unidentified alien (Magwit's Mystifying Hoop)|unusual alien]] the former eventually named [[Magwit's Mystifying Hoop]].{{FactFile|130|MAG 1-2|Magwit's Mystifying Hoop}} Bar-Kooda was amazed by Magwit's performance and wished to learn the secret behind the trick with the Mystifying Hoop. Magwit, however, feeling that he would simply be executed if he revealed how the device was operating, refused Bar-Kooda's request. Despite the defiance, the pirate did not kill Magwit and instead kept him aboard the ''Bloodstar'' for the following four [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. Bar-Kooda forced the magician to perform for the pirates' amusement while they committed acts that Magwit later described as "unspeakable" in front of him. At some point, Magwit escaped from the ''Bloodstar''. Bar-Kooda subsequently put out a [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] warrant for the magician's arrest.{{CSWECite|II|276|Magwit, Wim}} The pirate wanted the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to capture Magwit, and soon, by [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], Fett had indeed tracked the magician down{{FactFile|97|FET 29-32|Boba Fett}} to a [[Unidentified planet (Scrap-Yard City)|planet]] where Magwit was on a magic show tour. The [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] captured Magwit and [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] him aboard his [[Firespray-31-class patrol and attack craft/Legends|starship]], the [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']].{{CSWECite|III|167|''Slave I''}} ===Targeted by a Hutt=== {{Quote|I believe ''Orko'' has suffered heavy losses recently. Three of his ships have been laid waste by the pirate, ''Bar-Kooda''.''"
"''That's it! You've hit it! Bar-Kooda! Just the thing! But it won't be easy bringing that rogue to justice…there's only one who might succeed—send for ''Boba Fett''!|Ding and Gorga Desilijic Aarrpo|}} [[File:GorgaContactsFett-BarKooda.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Gorga Desilijic Aarrpo contacted Boba Fett and hired him to kill Bar-Kooda.]] Fett was at that point contacted by [[Gorga Desilijic Aarrpo/Legends|Gorga Desilijic Aarrpo]], the nephew of{{CSWECite|I|353|Gorga the Hutt}} the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]].{{CSWECite|II|130|Jabba the Hutt (Jabba Desilijic Tiure)}} Gorga had become smitten with the Hutt [[Anachro]], daughter of the wealthy [[Merchant/Legends|trader]] [[Orko]], and had been wondering what gift to present the merchant—who hated Gorga—so that Orko would bless a [[Marriage/Legends|marriage]] between Gorga and Anachro. After [[Ding (majordomo)|Ding]], Gorga's [[Majordomo/Legends|majordomo]],''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' notified his{{CSWECite|I|189|Ding}} master that Bar-Kooda had recently caused heavy losses to Orko by raiding three of the merchant's starships, Gorga had become convinced that the pirate's death would make a fine gift for Orko. To that end, the Hutt hired Fett to kill Bar-Kooda, although he was momentarily stunned—in light of the reward for the pirate's head being one hundred thousand [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]—by the bounty hunter's requested compensation of ''two'' hundred thousand credits, which Fett reasoned was justified by the challenge posed by Bar-Kooda. Learning about Magwit's history with the Herglic, Fett offered the magician freedom in return for him helping the bounty hunter [[Mission to the Bloodstar|capture]] the pirate—although Magwit only agreed to the deal when Fett threatened to kill him if he did not. ===Boarding the ''Bloodstar''=== {{Quote|Well, well! What strange twist of fate brings my old ''shipmate'' back to ''Bloodstar''?|Bar-Kooda, to Wim Magwit|}} [[File:MagwitBarKoodaReunited-BarKooda.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Wim Magwit and Bar-Kooda reunited aboard the ''Bloodstar'']] Magwit subsequently had Fett hide inside a large box and loaded all of his stage equipment, including the box, aboard one of the ''Slave I''{{'s}} [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]]. The pod, with Magwit aboard, was then left drifting in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] where a [[Bar-Kooda's scout ship|scout ship]] of the ''Bloodstar'' eventually discovered it. The [[Scout ship/Legends|scout ship]] brought the craft aboard Bar-Kooda's vessel, and the pirate captain himself witnessed the escape pod's arrival in a [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]]. When Magwit emerged from the escape pod, he claimed to have escaped from a ship aboard which he had a disagreement over his performance fees. Bar-Kooda threatened to kill the magician for his prior escape from him, but Magwit surprised the pirate by pretending that he welcomed the punishment from the captain. Bar-Kooda then ordered Magwit to perform one more show for him before he obliged the magician's "request" and killed him. ===The final show=== {{Quote|Now, captain, if you want to know how it works, just step right through and all the mysteries of the ''Hoop'' will be revealed!''"
"Through'' it, eh? I ain't so sure about that! What if you don't let me ''out'' again? What if you was to hold me for ''ransom'' or some connivin' trick like that? Maybe I'll try just a ''finger'' or two—test the waters, as it were!|Wim Magwit and Bar-Kooda|}} [[File:MagwitHoopBarKooda-BarKooda.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Wim Magwit performed the Mystifying Hoop trick for Bar-Kooda one last time.]] Magwit set up his equipment—including one of the [[matter transmitter]] frames of the Mystifying Hoop system as well as the box housing Fett—in the ''Bloodstar''{{'s}} cargo hold.''[[The Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]'' During Magwit's show before the pirates, Bar-Kooda hit one of his crewmembers for booing the magician during one of his preliminary acts, although the captain himself eventually called for Magwit to proceed to the Mystifying Hoop trick. The magician then transported a deck of playing cards through the hoop as a demonstration—and also to signal Fett, who had by that [[Time/Legends|time]] exited the box in the cargo hold. Magwit then invited Bar-Kooda to step through the hoop in order to learn how the device worked. At first testing the hoop by only putting his [[Limb/Legends|hand]] through it, Bar-Kooda soon stuck his head through the hoop's frame, only to learn that Fett was waiting for him on the other side of the teleportation system in the cargo hold. ===Encounter with a bounty hunter=== {{Quote|Rot my stump! B-''Boba Fett''!|Bar-Kooda, upon discovering Boba Fett at the other end of the Mystifying Hoop system|}} [[File:FettThrowsBarKooda-BarKooda.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Boba Fett defeated and captured Bar-Kooda.]] Fett promptly grabbed the pirate and pulled him through into the cargo hold, asking him to surrender and walk back to the ''Slave I'' with Fett. In response, Bar-Kooda warned the bounty hunter that the latter would perish aboard the ''Bloodstar'', to which Fett simply shot the Herglic in the torso with his [[Sacros K-11|blaster pistol]]. At that moment, Magwit also sent his show [[Bird/Legends|birds]] through the Mystifying Hoop, which distracted Fett, allowing Bar-Kooda to fire at him several [[Dart/Legends|darts]] from his [[gatling gauntlet]]. The Herglic then charged at Fett with his battle [[Axe/Legends|axe]], but the bounty hunter knocked the pirate out by firing his wrist-mounted [[ZX miniature flame projector/Legends|flamethrower]] at Bar-Kooda's head at close range. Carrying Bar-Kooda's unconscious body, Fett then rapidly began to make his way back to the escape pod in the ''Bloodstar''{{'s}} hangar. The bounty hunter was closely followed by Magwit, who had also escaped through the Mystifying Hoop from Bar-Kooda's crewmembers who were demanding their captain's return. After boarding the pod, Fett used the craft's [[Missile/Legends|missiles]] to blow a hole in the hangar's [[Blast door/Legends|blast doors]], causing [[explosive decompression]] that led to the deaths of many of the ''Bloodstar''{{'s}} crew.{{CSWECite|I|80|''Bloodstar''}} As the escape pod made its way back to the ''Slave I'', Fett remarked to Magwit that a very "unpleasant fate" awaited the barely conscious Bar-Kooda. ===Post-mortem=== {{Quote|My little gift to you, Orko…|Gorga Desilijic Aarrpo, on the dish prepared out of Bar-Kooda|}} [[File:GorgaBarKoodaDish-BarKooda.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Gorga Desilijic Aarrpo had a roasted dish prepared out of Bar-Kooda.]] Fett eventually killed Bar-Kooda and collected the bounty on him. In his [[Palace of Gorga the Hutt|palace]] on''[[Boba Fett: Murder Most Foul]]'' the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] planet''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' Tatooine, Gorga then organized a great feast to both honor Orko and celebrate the successful contract with Fett. For the [[Diet/Legends|dinner]], Bar-Kooda's body was turned into a delicious{{CSWECite|II|424|Orko (the Foultrader)}} roasted [[Foodstuff/Legends|entrée]] garnished with [[Fruit/Legends|fruit]], [[Vegetable/Legends|vegetables]], and [[Plant/Legends|greens]] and dressed with a [[Gravy/Legends|gravy]]. Deep cuts had been made in the Herglic's back, the remains of his hands and feet were covered with decorative manchettes, and an [[Apple/Legends|apple]] had been placed in the former pirate's [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]]. Presented with such a treat, Orko could not refuse the proposed [[Wedding of Gorga the Hutt and Anachro the H'uun|marriage]] between Gorga and Anachro, and all three Hutts tasted the Bar-Kooda dish during the meal. Shortly after Gorga and Anachro's wedding that same year, the [[Lizling]]{{CSWECite|I|90|Boz}} [[Boz (Lizling)|Boz]] learned of Bar-Kooda's death from a group of the Herglic's former crewmembers in a [[Unidentified Rutal IV bar|bar]] on Rutal IV. Boz relayed the news to Ry-Kooda, who visited the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] in order to learn more about his brother's demise. Upon learning that Bar-Kooda had been captured by Fett and subsequently prepared as a meal for the Hutts, Ry-Kooda flew into a rage and destroyed the building. With Boz constantly stoking the Herglic's thirst for vengeance, Ry-Kooda vowed to avenge his brother's death by killing Fett, Gorga,{{CSWECite|III|118|Ry-Kooda}} and Orko. To that end, he and Boz departed Rutal IV to search out Fett and the Hutts, and ultimately Ry-Kooda, although succeeding in killing Orko, himself perished at the hands of Fett. ==Personality and traits== ===A carnivorous pirate=== {{Quote|Bar-Kooda was a voracious carnivore—he had a reputation for ''eating'' his victims!|Wim Magwit|}} [[File:BarKoodaShootsSinger-BarKooda.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Bar-Kooda was a vicious pirate.]] A Herglic with a mottled forehead and chin who spoke a variation of [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] and resembled his brother, Ry-Kooda, visually, Bar-Kooda was a savage,''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' vicious, and impatient individual. Bar-Kooda described himself as an evil and hardhearted "spawn of a viper" but claimed that the act of denying the last request of an old shipmate would exceed his level of cruelty. Bar-Kooda's crew thought their captain was "bad enough as it [was]" so as to try and prevent him from becoming irritated. Although a [[Music/Legends|song]] about the impending death of a [[binggy]] on the planet''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' [[Gorm]] performed by a captured [[Unidentified singer|singer]] brought tears of sadness to Bar-Kooda's [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], he ultimately claimed he could only "take so much emotion." The Herglic further demonstrated his disregard for the lives of [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] at his mercy by executing the [[Musician/Legends|performer]]—a fate shared by other entertainers whose forced acts he did not enjoy—and commenting that the singer's remains would "do nicely for supper." The carnivorous pirate had a notorious reputation for eating his victims, with Wim Magwit's suggestion of carving up his [[Mold/Legends|moldy]] carcass and serving it to Bar-Kooda raw with a light garnish and large amounts of [[grotberry sauce]] making the Herglic hungry and bringing saliva to his mouth. ===Relationship with a magician=== {{Quote|I'd rather take my chances before an Imperial court than return to that monster!|Wim Magwit, on Bar-Kooda|}} [[File:MagwitBarKoodaAngry-BarKooda.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Despite Wim Magwit's defiance of him, Bar-Kooda kept the magician aboard the ''Bloodstar'' for four years.]] Bar-Kooda was very impressed with Magwit's magic tricks and acted in an affectionate manner toward the magician, although when the stage artist refused to reveal the secret behind his main trick, the pirate displayed his true vicious nature by roaring at him for refusing his orders. Once Magwit returned to Bar-Kooda after he had escaped from the pirate, the Herglic nearly immediately threatened to kill the magician. Bar-Kooda viewed Magwit as being full of surprises and did not tolerate his crewmembers disrespecting the magician. Magwit speculated that the pirate liked him in his own cruel way, but he himself characterized the Herglic as a "monster." Initially being suspicious of Magwit when the magician suggested that Bar-Kooda step through his Mystifying Hoop, the Herglic eventually came to suspect that the stage artist was hiding something. The pirate then discarded his previous caution, which ultimately had fatal consequences for the Herglic. ==Equipment== {{Quote|Bar-Kooda has a thousand pirates at his command—his ship, ''Bloodstar'', would give an Imperial battle-cruiser a close contest.|Boba Fett|}} Bar-Kooda wore a sleeveless [[Leather/Legends|leather]] [[Clothing/Legends|tunic]] and pants with protective kneepads and knee-high [[Boot/Legends|boots]]. Several pouches were attached to the pirate's [[Belt/Legends|belt]], and so was a [[Holster/Legends|holster]] housing a large [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] with a curved handle. A [[baldric]] slung over Bar-Kooda's right shoulder allowed him to carry a single-handed, single-edged battle axe on his back. Bar-Kooda wore a [[Vambrace/Legends|vambrace]] on his right forearm, while his left forearm housed a large gatling [[Gauntlet/Legends|gauntlet]] that terminated with a hook and could shoot lethal darts. The Herglic commanded a crew of a thousand pirates based out of a massive and well-armed ship, the ''Bloodstar'', which Boba Fett estimated could even prove to be in [[Space warfare/Legends|combat]] a challenge for [[Battlecruiser/Legends|battlecruisers]] of the Galactic Empire. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Bobafett_barkooda.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Bar-Kooda first appeared in the comic book ''Boba Fett: Bounty on Bar-Kooda''.]] Bar-Kooda was created for [[Boba Fett: Bounty on Bar-Kooda|''Bounty on Bar-Kooda'']], the first entry in the [[Star Wars: Boba Fett (comic series)|''Star Wars: Boba Fett'']]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54025/star_wars_boba_fett_-_bounty_on_bar-kooda_1995_1|text=Star Wars: Boba Fett - Bounty On Bar-Kooda (1995) #1}} series of one-shot [[Star Wars comic books|comic books]].{{StoryCite|story=The Bounty Hunter's Comic Book Career|book=Star Wars: Boba Fett}} The issue was written by [[John Wagner]], illustrated by [[Cam Kennedy]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[December 5]], [[1995]]. Prior to the character's appearance in ''Bounty on Bar-Kooda'', he was first mentioned in the [[October]] 1995''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]'' reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' by [[Andy Mangels]]. In his article "[[Just Who Is Boba Fett?]]," included in the{{StoryCite|story=Just Who Is Boba Fett?|book=Star Wars: Boba Fett}} [[April]] [[1998]][[Star Wars Galaxy Collector 1|''Star Wars Galaxy Collector'' 1]] one-shot magazine ''[[Star Wars: Boba Fett]]'', Mangels listed the popular bounty hunter Boba Fett's capture of Bar-Kooda, as depicted in ''Bounty on Bar-Kooda'', as one of the former character's "top 10 [bounty] hunts." In its entry for the character Anachro, the [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] misidentified Bar-Kooda as a bounty hunter.{{DB|character|anachro|Anachro the H'uun}} In addition, despite ''Bounty on Bar-Kooda'' depicting Bar-Kooda as being captured alive by Fett, the [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 97|ninety-seventh issue]] of [[De Agostini]]'s ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' magazine—written by [[Iain Lowson]]{{LinkedIn|url=in/iainlowson|text=Iain Lowson|archivefile=File:IainLowson-LinkedIn.png}} and published around [[November 5]], [[2003]]{{FFdates|97}}—incorrectly claimed that Fett killed Bar-Kooda within "seconds" of their brawl. The ''Fact File'' issue also established that Fett had specifically been hired to kill the pirate. In [[2008]], ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' confirmed that Bar-Kooda did indeed perish at the bounty hunter's hands, which this article assumes occurred following the pirate's capture. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Bar-Kooda}} *''[[Boba Fett: Bounty on Bar-Kooda]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Boba Fett: When the Fat Lady Swings]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Boba Fett: Murder Most Foul]]'' {{Imo}} ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' {{1stm}} *{{GalaxyCite|6|ComicScan|ComicScan: Pulp Faction}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]'' *{{StoryCite|story=Just Who Is Boba Fett?|book=Star Wars: Boba Fett}} *{{StoryCite|story=The Bounty Hunter's Comic Book Career|book=Star Wars: Boba Fett}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030911hutt|text=Hutt! Hutt! Hutt!}} *{{FactFile|97|FET 29-32|Boba Fett}} *{{FactFile|130|MAG 1-2|Magwit's Mystifying Hoop}} *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|anachro|Anachro the H'uun}} ==Notes and references== {{ScrollBox|content= {{Reflist}} }} {{Interlang |de=Bar-Kooda |pl=Bar-Kooda }} [[Category:Food]] [[Category:Herglics]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Pirates]]
